item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This is an anomaly from Gamers Against Weed, so it should be linked to the hub.

In addition, it also mentions a website used to host "holy-heck.rtf". I propose linking SCP-3772, another GAW anomaly, to this website. Realized this doesn't work.

Editing this:

I also know that you're assigned to research other "Misters" by Gamers Against Weed.

To this:

I also know that you're assigned to research other "Misters" by Gamers Against Weed.

Editing this:

7. I can't speak to that, but perhaps this document will suffice: http://███-██████████.███/███-████/files/holyheck.rtf

To this:

7. I can't speak to that, but perhaps this document will suffice: http://███-██████████.███1/███-████/files/holyheck.rtf

Footnotes
1. This domain name was previously associated with SCP-3772.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While SCP-3024 was active in the "███ ██████████" community under the account name "Trouble Greg", a pre-existing account operated by a Foundation staff member was volunteered for the purpose of privately communicating with SCP-3024.

That's the same number of characters as "SCP Foundation". :O

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bonus points for presenting these in the same order as in Dante's Purgatorio (though maybe that's common and I just missed it). Minus a point because far-right=gluttony doesn't make sense and is just there to fill out the list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, wow, it's the first GAW content I've enjoyed! Good job!

I have consistently thought that we need more computing-related SCPs,* and this is a great example of an anomalous program (in my interpretation!). To me, the sense of satisfaction it reports experiencing is simply an advanced form of fitness function.

Altogether this is a good exploration of anomaly in the modern world, and I should like to see more in this vein.

* Entirely coincidentally to my own profession as a computer scientist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a thing that's used to convey or at least hint at a story. Unfortunately, neither the thing nor the story is particularly interesting. Given the timing, it's impossible for me not to associate this with site-related drama, which is almost always (including this time) serves to the detriment of the article.

-1, although the line "I'm not a human for the same reason you're not a crocodile" is pretty great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3024's consistently describe misconduct which actually occurred.

Hmm.

Otherwise, this is a pretty neat article. I don't really like how vague our Little Mister here is with the ending, but what can you do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how you interpreted the sins and didn't make the Mister itself the sinner

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll address those points once I get home from work. Thanks for bringing them up, and I'm glad you liked the article. I should also mention that I'll add some examples of its work to the article while I'm at it.

It is a reference to the Box Ghost, yes. I told chat I would take the first title suggestion I got, and I believe stormfallen supplied this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very good, but I have two very minor issues with wording.

Evidence by SCP-3024 consistently describes actual misconduct.

I assume you meant to say that the actions described in the evidence actually did occur, but it's worded awkwardly. Perhaps say, "SCP-3024's accusations always describe misconduct which actually occurred."

Accusations made by SCP-3024 frequently result in the punishment, demotion, and/or expulsion of the offender.

I'm assuming that this is simply the natural consequence of the revelations SCP-3024 brings to light, but it could be interpreted as being part of its anomalous effects. Maybe put a footnote here saying, "This is not a part of SCP-3024's anomalous effects, and happens naturally as a result of its actions."

Other than that, I very much enjoyed this. Was the title meant to be a reference to the Box Ghost from Danny Phantom?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wherever it's non-obvious, that's where I (And the creators in-universe) got lazy and decided to not do a 1:1 correlation any more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the article, but I have a quick question: which sins match up to which categories?

Some are obvious (sexual harassment is obviously Lust, dereliction is obviously Sloth, plagiarism is probably Envy), but some others I'm not really sure which fits the best. (Is misuse of power Pride or Greed? Is Wrath being a Nazi, or stalking/doxing? And which one is Gluttony supposed to be?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does a thing and it does the thing really well, but past that I don't really latch onto anything else. It sort of reminds me of another skip (that I can't recall its number atm) that goes on online forums to mess about with the people on, but this doesn't interest me. Could have been more interesting had there been any examples of unique or insightful ways in which the skip exposed nazis, harassers, etc., but not as it is right now. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my god, Dr. Prasad, you can't just ask people why they're not human! +1

item.title: *dabs*
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to everyone who reviewed this! The image was made by myself, incorporating some public-domain imagery.

There was a correspondence between these sins and the real deadly sins for like, 4-5 of them, but past a certain point I just went "fuck it". You can assume that this happened in-universe, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Take your first upvote you beautiful man
